g.text: WAL: Concurrent Readers + Writer vs. Rollback Journal Serialization
WAL appends to a separate file; readers use end_frame snapshots for isolation; no reader ever blocks a writer.
W acquires LOCK_EX on db file
T5: R1 and R2 proceed (serial, sequential)
1 writer OR N readers — never both
T1: Writer W — BEGIN WRITE TXN
R1 snapshot: end_frame = 0 (sees no WAL frames yet)
T2: R1 reads page 42 wal_find_frame(42, end_frame=0)
WAL index: page42 → frame[0] | frame[0] index 0 ≥ end_frame 0 → NOT visible to R1
R1: wal_find_frame returns -1 → reads page 42 from main DB (pre-write snapshot)
T3: W appends frame[1] (page_no=77, COMMIT db_size=100)
R2 snapshot: end_frame = 2 (sees frame[0] and frame[1], txn committed)
WAL index: page42 → frame[0], page77 → frame[1] | both frames < end_frame 2 → VISIBLE to R2
T4: R1 reads page 42 (snap=0) → main DB [pre-commit value]
T4: R2 reads page 42 (snap=2) → WAL frame[0] [post-commit value]
T4: W continues appending next txn frames — no mutex needed
1 writer AND N readers simultaneously — no file-level lock contention
W acquires WAL write lock (only 1 writer at a time)
